creator: peter.thoenen@protonmail.com
text:
Nils Vogels wrote:
> maintainer, so I submitted the upgrade to 0.1.2.14, didnt mean to step
> on anyone's toes here ;)

No biggie ... it is just a gut reflex you get as a maintainer of popular 
ports ... e.g. you get folk nagging you 15 minutes after a new release 
or multiple people all submitting PR to update your port ... especially 
obnoxious when you have already submitted a PR yourself and just waiting 
a committer to commit it.

As you noticed, some guy was griping on the or-talk list because there 
was no package and for some reason he couldn't query the PR database (to 
see an update was submitted), couldn't email the maintainer (me), and 
couldn't wait ... folk like that wear on you after awhile

> Since tor releases quite often, I think leaving it up to ports@ may
> cause the port to be left behind. 

I concur

> I use security/tor and wouldn't mind
> maintaining it.

Works for me .. if you want you got it.

Martin: I was going to drop it anyways, how about:

-maintainer=peter.thoenen@yahoo.com
+maintainer=nivo@is-root.com

As my last maintainer update, approved by me ;)

BTW: Not a nitty gritty code guy Nils so if I see a tor port improvement 
by you, I will shameless steal it and import it into tor-devel :)

Was a gripe I always had with the old -stable and -devel maintainers as 
the -stable and -devel ports were entirely out-of-sync (to include 
rc.substr's, UID, torrc mods, etc etc) .. will try and stay synced w/ 
you where it make senses.
